name: Constitution of Nepal
description: The supreme law of Nepal establishing the fundamental principles of governance, constitutional structure, rights and duties of citizens, distribution of state power across federal, provincial, and local levels, constitutional bodies, amendment procedures, and transitional arrangements following the adoption of the federal democratic republican system.

contents.title: Preliminary
contents.description: Defines the foundational characteristics of the Nepali state, including sovereignty, state authority, constitutional supremacy, national symbols, citizenship principles, and the basic nature of Nepal as a federal democratic republic.

contents.title: Citizenship
contents.description: Specifies the types of Nepali citizenship, eligibility criteria, acquisition and termination of citizenship, rights of citizens, and provisions related to citizenship by descent, naturalization, and gender equality in citizenship matters.

contents.title: Fundamental Rights and Duties
contents.description: Enumerates enforceable fundamental rights such as equality, freedom, justice, social security, education, health, and constitutional remedies, along with the fundamental duties of citizens toward the nation.

contents.title: Directive Principles, Policies and Obligations of the State
contents.description: Outlines non-justiciable guiding principles and state policies aimed at social justice, economic development, inclusion, good governance, environmental protection, and national interest.

contents.title: Structure of State and Distribution of State Power
contents.description: Defines Nepal's federal structure, the division of powers among federation, states, and local levels, and principles governing cooperative federalism and jurisdictional authority.

contents.title: President and Vice-President
contents.description: Details the roles, powers, election procedures, terms, and constitutional responsibilities of the President and Vice-President as ceremonial heads of state.

contents.title: Federal Executive
contents.description: Covers the formation, powers, and functions of the Council of Ministers, the Prime Minister, executive accountability, and federal governance operations.

contents.title: Federal Legislature
contents.description: Describes the composition, powers, privileges, and functioning of the Federal Parliament, including the House of Representatives and National Assembly.

contents.title: Federal Legislative Procedures
contents.description: Explains the legislative process at the federal level, including bill introduction, passage, assent, joint sittings, and parliamentary procedures.

contents.title: Federal Financial Procedures
contents.description: Regulates federal budgeting, taxation authority, public finance management, audit requirements, and expenditure authorization.

contents.title: Judiciary
contents.description: Establishes the judicial system, including the Supreme Court, High Courts, and District Courts, judicial independence, jurisdiction, and constitutional review.

contents.title: Attorney General
contents.description: Defines the appointment, powers, duties, and advisory role of the Attorney General as the chief legal advisor to the Government of Nepal.

contents.title: State Executive
contents.description: Specifies the structure and authority of provincial executives, including the Chief Minister, Council of Ministers, and their responsibilities.

contents.title: State Legislature
contents.description: Details the composition, powers, and functioning of Provincial Assemblies and their legislative authority.

contents.title: State Legislative Procedures
contents.description: Explains legislative processes within provinces, including bill procedures, assent, and internal assembly operations.

contents.title: State Financial Procedures
contents.description: Regulates provincial-level public finance, taxation powers, budget formulation, and expenditure control.

contents.title: Local Executive
contents.description: Defines the structure, authority, and responsibilities of local governments, including village and municipal executives.

contents.title: Local Financial Procedures
contents.description: Specifies financial management, revenue authority, and budgeting processes at the local government level.

contents.title: Interrelations between Federation, State and Local Level
contents.description: Addresses coordination, cooperation, dispute resolution, and power-sharing mechanisms among federal, provincial, and local governments.

contents.title: Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
contents.description: Establishes the anti-corruption constitutional body responsible for investigating misuse of public office and authority.

contents.title: Auditor General
contents.description: Defines the role of the Auditor General in auditing public accounts, ensuring financial accountability and transparency.

contents.title: Public Service Commission
contents.description: Covers the constitutional body responsible for merit-based recruitment, examinations, and regulation of public service appointments.

contents.title: Election Commission
contents.description: Establishes the authority responsible for conducting free, fair, and impartial elections and referendums.

contents.title: National Human Rights Commission
contents.description: Defines the mandate to protect, promote, and monitor human rights and investigate violations.

contents.title: National Natural Resources and Fiscal Commission
contents.description: Regulates equitable distribution of natural resources, fiscal transfers, and revenue-sharing among government levels.

contents.title: Other Commissions
contents.description: Establishes additional constitutional commissions addressing inclusion, social justice, and protection of marginalized groups.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to National Security
contents.description: Covers national security policy, control of armed forces, and mechanisms for safeguarding sovereignty and territorial integrity.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Political Parties
contents.description: Regulates formation, registration, conduct, and constitutional compliance of political parties.

contents.title: Emergency Power
contents.description: Defines conditions, procedures, and limitations for declaring a state of emergency and suspending certain rights.

contents.title: Amendment to the Constitution
contents.description: Specifies the procedures, requirements, and limitations for amending the Constitution of Nepal.

contents.title: Miscellaneous
contents.description: Contains supplementary constitutional provisions, interpretations, and administrative matters not covered elsewhere.

contents.title: Transitional Provisions
contents.description: Provides temporary arrangements for institutional transition, continuity of laws, and implementation following constitutional adoption.

name: The National Civil (Code) Act, 2017 (2074)
description: A comprehensive legal framework enacted to amend and consolidate the civil laws of Nepal. It regulates matters concerning general legal principles, civil rights, natural and legal persons, family law, property rights, contracts, other liabilities including torts, and private international law.

contents.title: General Provisions
contents.description: Covers the short title, commencement, definitions of key terms, and the saving of special legal provisions.

contents.title: General Principles of Civil Law
contents.description: Establishes foundational legal principles, such as ignorance of the law is not an excuse, liability for wrongdoings, and maintaining good neighborhood.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Civil Rights
contents.description: Guarantees equality before the law, non-discrimination, fundamental freedoms, the right to privacy, and provisions for compensation for civil rights violations.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Natural Persons
contents.description: Details the recognition of natural persons, the right to a name, legal competency, handling of minors and incompetent persons, and the presumption of death.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Legal Persons
contents.description: Covers the incorporation, legal competency, management, operation, and liabilities of body corporates and legal entities.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Bankruptcy of Natural Persons
contents.description: Outlines the procedures for initiating bankruptcy proceedings, asset management, priority order for settling debts, and the termination of bankruptcy status.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Marriage
contents.description: Regulates the freedom of marriage, conditions for valid marriages, void and voidable marriages, and the mandatory registration of marriages.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Consequences of Marriage
contents.description: Details the mutual obligations, cohabitation requirements, and shared household management responsibilities of husband and wife.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Divorce
contents.description: Sets out the conditions and procedures for divorce by mutual consent or petition, including rules for the partition of property and alimony.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Relationship of Mother, Father and Son and Daughter
contents.description: Covers the determination of paternity and maternity, birth registration, custody arrangements, and the obligations for the care and maintenance of children.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Parental Authority
contents.description: Defines the rights, responsibilities, and duties of parents over their children, including restrictions on and the termination of parental authority.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Guardianship
contents.description: Details the priority order, appointment, disqualification, rights, and duties of guardians for incompetent or quasi-competent persons.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Curatorship
contents.description: Outlines the appointment, qualifications, and responsibilities of curators managing the affairs and properties of minors or persons of unsound mind.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Adoption
contents.description: Specifies the conditions, legal procedures, and reciprocal rights and obligations involved in domestic adoption.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Inter-country Adoption
contents.description: Regulates the adoption of Nepali children by foreigners, including the formation and role of the Inter-country Adoption Board and necessary permissions.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Partition
contents.description: Provides rules for identifying coparceners, ensuring equal entitlement to property, executing partition deeds, and managing the separation of joint family property.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Succession
contents.description: Details the order of preference for heirs, the acceptance or rejection of succession rights, and the handling of properties belonging to deceased foreigners.

contents.title: General Provisions Relating to Property
contents.description: Defines property and categorizes it into movable, immovable, private, common, joint, community, public, government, and trust properties.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Ownership and Possession
contents.description: Explains the rights of property owners, the lawful acquisition of possessory rights, and the rules governing adverse possession.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Uses of Property
contents.description: Sets restrictions on encroaching upon neighbors, rules for building structures, and necessary safety measures for property usage.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Cultivation, Use and Registration of Land
contents.description: Governs the cultivation and use of land, water and ditch rights, and the mandatory requirements for land transmission and registration.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Government, Public and Community Properties
contents.description: Defines government, public, and community properties, outlining the obligations to protect them and prohibiting unauthorized private registration or use.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Trusts
contents.description: Regulates the establishment, registration, operation, and management of public and private trusts, including the duties and liabilities of trustees.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Usufruct
contents.description: Covers the creation, validity, and specific rights and obligations involved when a person is granted the temporary right to use and derive benefit from another's property.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Servitudes
contents.description: Details the rules allowing a property owner to use another's property for specific necessary purposes, such as passage, water flow, or utility lines.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to House Rent
contents.description: Outlines the mutual obligations of landlords and tenants, requirements for rent agreements, maintenance rules, and legal procedures for eviction.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Donation and Gift
contents.description: Specifies the conditions under which donations and gifts are valid, void, or voidable, including rules for gifts with testamentary effect.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Transfer and Acquisition of Property
contents.description: Governs the legal rules and execution of deeds required for selling, exchanging, or otherwise transferring property ownership rights.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Mortgage of Immovable Property
contents.description: Sets the conditions for mortgages with or without possession, the rights and duties of mortgagees, and the procedures for property redemption.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Pre-emption of Immovable Property
contents.description: Defines the legal rights of heirs and neighbors to pre-empt (purchase) sold immovable property, and the petition procedures for exercising this right.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Approval of Registration of Deeds
contents.description: Lists the specific deeds that require mandatory registration, the registration procedures, and the associated fees.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Transactions
contents.description: Covers general rules for financial transactions, borrowing loans, the execution of transaction deeds, and legal limits on interest rates.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Obligations
contents.description: Establishes how legal obligations are created (e.g., by law, contract, tort) and the general rules governing their fulfillment.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Formation of Contracts
contents.description: Details the requirements for a valid contract, including the competence of parties, the mechanisms of offer and acceptance, and contingent contracts.

contents.title: Validity of Contracts
contents.description: Defines the specific circumstances that render a contract void, voidable (such as those involving coercion, fraud, or undue influence), or unenforceable.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Performance of Contracts
contents.description: Provides rules regarding the designated time, place, and manner for performing contractual obligations, and circumstances excusing performance.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Breach of Contracts and Remedies
contents.description: Outlines the consequences of a contract breach, the methods for assessing compensation, and the conditions for claiming specific performance.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Contracts of Sales of Goods
contents.description: Regulates the sale of goods, covering price determination, payment terms, quality conditions, transfer of ownership, and delivery rules.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Contracts of Guarantee
contents.description: Details the obligations and liabilities of sureties or guarantors, conditions for their discharge, and the rules surrounding contracts of indemnity.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Contracts of Bailment
contents.description: Covers the respective duties of bailors and bailees concerning the care, permitted use, and mandatory return of bailed goods.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Contracts of Pledge or Deposit
contents.description: Sets the rules for providing and returning goods as collateral or deposit, and the rights of the pledgee to sell the collateral upon default.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Contracts of Agency
contents.description: Regulates the appointment, rights, duties, and liabilities of agents, sub-agents, and their principals in business transactions.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Contracts of Carriage of Goods
contents.description: Defines the obligations, rights, and limits of liability for carriers engaged in the transportation of goods.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Contracts of Lease
contents.description: Outlines the rules for leasing goods or property, including maintenance responsibilities, payment of lease rent, and conditions for termination.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Hire-purchase Contracts
contents.description: Details the required terms, conditions, transfer of ownership, and specific termination rules governing hire-purchase agreements.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Wages
contents.description: Covers general employment rules, wage determination, standard working hours, and specific legal protections for domestic helpers.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Indirect or Quasi-Contracts
contents.description: Regulates obligations that arise without a formal contract, such as the voluntary management of another's property and rights to reimbursement.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Unjust Enrichment
contents.description: Provides the legal rules requiring the return of benefits, goods, or money that a person has obtained by mistake or without legal justification.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Torts
contents.description: Establishes liabilities and the right to compensation for damages caused by negligence, trespass, or the actions of dependents, animals, or structures.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Liability for Defective Products
contents.description: Defines the responsibilities and liabilities of producers and sellers for any loss or damage caused to consumers by defective goods or services.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Private International Law
contents.description: Governs cases involving foreign parties or elements, including rules on legal capacity, international marriage and divorce, property rights, and the jurisdiction of Nepali courts.

name: The National Criminal Procedure (Code) Act, 2017
description: A procedural law enacted to amend and consolidate the laws governing investigation, prosecution, filing, proceedings, hearing, adjudication, and execution of judgments in criminal cases in Nepal.

contents.title: Preliminary
contents.description: Establishes the short title, commencement date, and key definitions used throughout the Code, including court, offence, investigating authority, criminal case, and government attorney.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Information on Commission of, and Investigation into, Offences
contents.description: Covers the procedures for receiving information about offences, initiating investigations, conducting searches and seizures, gathering evidence, and the duties and powers of investigating authorities.

contents.title: Prosecution and Filing of Cases
contents.description: Details the rules governing the prosecution of offences, the role of government attorneys, filing of charge-sheets, and the procedures for initiating cases in courts.

contents.title: Jurisdiction of Court
contents.description: Defines the territorial and subject-matter jurisdiction of courts at different levels to hear and adjudicate criminal cases.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Preliminary Proceedings of Cases
contents.description: Covers the initial procedural steps once a case is filed, including scrutiny of complaints, preliminary hearings, and notices to accused persons.

contents.title: Warrant for Arrest and Summons
contents.description: Specifies the conditions, authority, and procedures for issuing warrants for arrest and summons to accused persons and witnesses, including provisions for absconding persons.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Detention, Bail and Guarantee
contents.description: Regulates the lawful detention of accused persons, the conditions and procedures for granting bail, types of guarantees, and provisions for detention during investigation and trial.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Appointed Date
contents.description: Governs the scheduling of hearing dates, fixing of procedural timelines, and consequences of non-appearance by parties before the court.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Attorney
contents.description: Outlines the rights of accused persons to legal representation, the role of appointed and defense attorneys, and provisions relating to legal aid.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Transfer and Adjournment of Cases
contents.description: Provides grounds and procedures for transferring cases between courts, granting adjournments, and managing case scheduling in the interest of justice.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Examination of Evidence
contents.description: Covers the rules for examining witnesses, recording statements, admissibility of evidence, cross-examination, and the examination of experts and documents.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Withdrawal, Compromise and Mediation of Cases
contents.description: Sets out the conditions under which cases may be withdrawn, settled through compromise between parties, or referred to mediation, including compoundable offences.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Proceedings, Hearing of, and Judgments in, Cases
contents.description: Regulates the conduct of trials, the hearing of arguments, recording of judgments, sentencing, and the pronouncement of decisions by courts.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Appeal and Reference for Sanction
contents.description: Defines the right of appeal against court decisions, the appellate process, timelines for filing appeals, and provisions for referring certain matters for sanction to higher authorities.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Execution of Judgments
contents.description: Covers the procedures for enforcing court judgments, including the execution of sentences of imprisonment, fines, forfeiture, and the realization of compensation awarded to victims.

contents.title: Miscellaneous
contents.description: Contains supplementary provisions including power to frame rules, provisions relating to limitation periods, international legal cooperation, and transitional arrangements.

name: The Electronic Transactions Act, 2063 (2008)
description: An Act to provide legal recognition, validity, and security to electronic records and digital signatures, regulate certifying authorities, govern electronic commerce and government use of electronic records, address computer-related offences, and establish IT tribunals for adjudication.

contents.title: Preliminary
contents.description: Sets out the short title, commencement, territorial extent, and comprehensive definitions of key terms including electronic record, digital signature, computer, certifying authority, subscriber, controller, and related concepts.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Electronic Record and Digital Signature
contents.description: Establishes the legal recognition and validity of electronic records and digital signatures, conditions for their secure use, and requirements for safe storage and submission of electronic records in place of originals.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Dispatch, Receipt and Acknowledgement of Electronic Records
contents.description: Governs attribution of electronic records to originators, procedures for acknowledgement of receipt, and rules determining the time and place of dispatch and receipt of electronic records.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Controller and Certifying Authority
contents.description: Establishes the Controller of Certifying Authorities, defines functions and powers of the Controller, and regulates the licensing, renewal, suspension, revocation, and performance audit of certifying authorities.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Digital Signature and Certificates
contents.description: Covers the issuance, suspension, and revocation of digital signature certificates by licensed certifying authorities, and the public notice requirements for such actions.

contents.title: Functions, Duties and Rights of Subscriber
contents.description: Defines the obligations of subscribers in generating key pairs, accepting certificates, retaining private keys securely, and depositing private keys to the Controller when required for national security or law enforcement purposes.

contents.title: Electronic Record and Government Use of Digital Signature
contents.description: Authorizes the Government of Nepal to publish official documents in electronic form, accept electronic filings, and use digital signatures in government offices and public institutions.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Network Service
contents.description: Defines the liabilities of network service providers acting as intermediaries and limits their liability for third-party content unless they have knowledge of its illegal nature.

contents.title: Offences Relating to Computer
contents.description: Creates criminal offences for unauthorized access, damage to computer systems, piracy of source code, publication of illegal electronic content, breach of confidentiality, computer fraud, submission of false statements, and abetment of computer offences, with associated penalties.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Information Technology Tribunal
contents.description: Establishes a three-member Information Technology Tribunal to adjudicate computer offences, including qualifications of members, terms of office, vacancy conditions, staffing, and procedural rules.

contents.title: Provisions Relating to Information Technology Appellate Tribunal
contents.description: Establishes the IT Appellate Tribunal to hear appeals against decisions of the Tribunal and the Controller, with provisions for member qualifications, tenure, vacancies, and procedures.

contents.title: Miscellaneous
contents.description: Contains supplementary provisions including party agreements on electronic record procedures, government directives to the Controller, limitation periods for complaints, government as plaintiff in offence cases, compensation recovery, exclusions from the Act's application, and rule-making powers.

name: Income Tax Act, 2058 (2002)
description: The principal legislation governing income tax in Nepal, enacted to amend and consolidate the law on income tax. It covers the tax base, computation, exemptions, deductions, special provisions for entities and sectors, international tax, withholding, assessment, collection, appeals, penalties, and miscellaneous administrative matters.

contents.title: Preliminary
contents.description: Provides the short title, commencement, territorial scope, and extensive definitions of key terms used throughout the Act, including person, income, entity, employment, investment, business, and related concepts.

contents.title: Tax Bases
contents.description: Defines the taxable persons, types of taxable income (employment, business, investment), the residence rules determining tax liability, and the basic framework for calculating assessable income.

contents.title: Computation of Tax
contents.description: Specifies how taxable income from business, employment, and investment is computed, including the treatment of capital gains, and sets out the applicable tax rates.

contents.title: Exemptible Amounts and Other Exemptions
contents.description: Lists amounts exempt from income tax including income of certain entities, specific payments, and other categories of exempt income.

contents.title: Deductible Amounts
contents.description: Enumerates the expenses and amounts deductible in computing business income, including general business expenses, depreciation, research and development costs, and provisions for bad debts.

contents.title: Tax Accounting and Time
contents.description: Prescribes the income year, accounting methods (cash vs. accrual), translation of foreign currency amounts, and rules for timing of income recognition and deductions.

contents.title: Quantification, Allocation and Characterization of Amounts
contents.description: Covers the rules for market value determination, allocation of amounts between related parties, anti-avoidance provisions, and characterization of income in transactions involving related or associated persons.

contents.title: Assessment of Net Profit from Property and Liability
contents.description: Governs the computation of income from the disposal of assets and settlement of liabilities, cost basis rules, recognition of gains and losses, and treatment of depreciable assets.

contents.title: Special Provisions for Entity
contents.description: Contains special tax rules applicable to entities, including companies and partnerships, covering distribution of profits, dividend treatment, and the taxation of entity-level transactions.

contents.title: Special Provisions on Banking and Insurance Business
contents.description: Provides sector-specific rules for the computation of taxable income of banks, financial institutions, and insurance companies, including provisions for loan loss reserves and insurance technical reserves.

contents.title: Special Provisions on Retirement Saving
contents.description: Regulates the tax treatment of contributions to and payments from approved retirement funds, and defines the conditions for tax concessions on retirement savings and benefits.

contents.title: International Tax
contents.description: Covers the taxation of non-resident persons and income with an international element, rules on permanent establishments, foreign tax credits, thin capitalization, transfer pricing, and controlled foreign corporations.

contents.title: Records and Information Collection
contents.description: Requires taxpayers and third parties to maintain accounts and records, and empowers tax officers to access premises, inspect records, and collect information for tax administration purposes.

contents.title: Payment of Tax
contents.description: Specifies the time, place, and mode for payment of income tax, including rules for the payment of advance tax and withholding tax obligations.

contents.title: Withholding on Payment
contents.description: Establishes the withholding tax regime, requiring persons making specified payments (employment income, dividends, interest, rent, service fees, contract payments) to withhold and remit tax on behalf of recipients.

contents.title: Installment
contents.description: Provides the rules for payment of income tax in installments during the income year, including the calculation of installment amounts and due dates.

contents.title: Income Return and Assessment of Tax
contents.description: Regulates the filing of income tax returns, self-assessment, departmental assessment, amended assessments, and the procedures for determining tax liability by tax officers.

contents.title: Collection, Remission and Refund of Tax
contents.description: Covers the enforcement of tax collection, recovery of tax from third parties, security for tax, provisions for remission of tax in hardship cases, and the refund of excess tax paid.

contents.title: Review and Appeal
contents.description: Establishes the administrative and judicial review mechanisms for disputing tax assessments, including internal review by the Tax Department, appeal to the Revenue Tribunal, and further appeal to courts.

contents.title: Fees and Interest
contents.description: Prescribes interest charges on late payment of tax, fees for late filing of returns, and other financial penalties for non-compliance with procedural obligations.

contents.title: Offence and Punishment
contents.description: Defines tax-related criminal offences including tax evasion, filing false returns, and obstruction of tax administration, along with applicable fines and imprisonment penalties.

contents.title: Miscellaneous
contents.description: Contains supplementary provisions including the establishment and powers of the Inland Revenue Department, taxpayer identification numbers, confidentiality of information, power to frame rules, and transitional arrangements.

name: The National Penal (Code) Act, 2017
description: A comprehensive criminal code enacted to amend and consolidate the laws relating to criminal offences in Nepal. It covers general provisions on criminal justice principles, punishment, and conspiracy in Part 1; a wide range of substantive criminal offences against the state, public order, persons, property, and morals in Part 2; and offences against individual privacy and prestige in Part 3.

contents.title: Part-1, Chapter-1: Preliminary
contents.description: Provides the short title, commencement, and foundational definitions used throughout the Code, including offence, punishment, person, public servant, and related terms.

contents.title: Part-1, Chapter-2: General Principles of Criminal Justice
contents.description: Establishes core principles of criminal liability including the requirement that no act required by law is an offence, the doctrine of mens rea, general exceptions such as self-defence, necessity, consent, mistake of fact, acts of minors and persons of unsound mind, and other circumstances excluding criminal responsibility.

contents.title: Part-1, Chapter-3: Criminal Conspiracy, Attempt, Abetment and Accomplice
contents.description: Defines and penalises criminal conspiracy, attempt to commit offences, abetment of offences, and the liability of accomplices, including rules on how punishment is apportioned among co-offenders.

contents.title: Part-1, Chapter-4: Gravity of Offence — Aggravating and Mitigating Factors
contents.description: Sets out the factors that courts must consider in determining the gravity of an offence, including aggravating circumstances that increase punishment and mitigating circumstances that may reduce it.

contents.title: Part-1, Chapter-5: Provisions Relating to Punishment and Interim Relief
contents.description: Defines the types of punishments applicable under the Code (imprisonment, fines, forfeiture, etc.), rules for sentencing, concurrent and consecutive sentences, conversion of punishment, and provisions for interim relief to victims pending final judgment.

contents.title: Part-2, Chapter-1: Offences against the State
contents.description: Criminalises acts against Nepal's sovereignty, territorial integrity, and constitutional order, including treason, waging war against the state, espionage, sedition, and offences relating to national security and armed forces.

contents.title: Part-2, Chapter-2: Offences against Public Tranquility
contents.description: Covers offences that disturb public order and peace, including unlawful assembly, rioting, affray, incitement to violence, and acts promoting enmity or hatred among communities.

contents.title: Part-2, Chapter-3: Offences Relating to Contempt of Authority of Public Servants
contents.description: Defines offences involving obstruction, assault, or use of criminal force against public servants, disobedience of lawful orders, escape from custody, giving false information to authorities, and offences relating to evidence and court proceedings.

contents.title: Part-2, Chapter-5: Offences against Public Interest, Health, Safety, Convenience and Morals
contents.description: Criminalises acts harmful to the public at large, including adulteration of food and drugs, public nuisance, obscenity, gambling, offences relating to public health and safety, and immoral trafficking.

contents.title: Part-2, Chapter-6: Offences Relating to Arms and Ammunitions
contents.description: Prohibits the unlicensed possession, manufacture, sale, and use of arms, ammunition, and other weapons, and prescribes penalties for violations.

contents.title: Part-2, Chapter-7: Offences Relating to Explosives
contents.description: Covers offences involving the unlawful manufacture, possession, use, transport, and sale of explosive substances, and offences causing harm or damage through explosives.

contents.title: Part-2, Chapter-8: Offences against National and Public Heritages
contents.description: Protects national heritage, archaeological sites, monuments, and public property from damage, destruction, theft, and illegal export.

contents.title: Part-2, Chapter-9: Offences Relating to Religion
contents.description: Penalises acts that offend religious sentiments, desecrate places of worship or religious objects, disturb religious assemblies, and promote religious hatred or enmity.

contents.title: Part-2, Chapter-11: Offences Relating to Marriage
contents.description: Defines criminal offences in the context of marriage, including bigamy, fraudulent marriage, child marriage, and offences relating to dowry and marital rights.

contents.title: Part-2, Chapter-12: Offences Relating to Human Body
contents.description: Covers offences including murder, culpable homicide, infanticide, causing death by negligence, and other offences resulting in the unlawful taking of human life.

contents.title: Part-2, Chapter-13: Offences against Protection of Pregnancy
contents.description: Addresses criminal liability for unlawful abortion and other offences relating to the protection of pregnancy, including the conditions under which termination is lawful.

contents.title: Part-2, Chapter-14: Offences Relating to Hurt or Grievous Hurt
contents.description: Defines and penalises causing hurt and grievous hurt to a person, including acid attacks, torture, and acts causing permanent disfigurement or disability.

contents.title: Part-2, Chapter-15: Offences Relating to Unlawful Detention
contents.description: Penalises the wrongful confinement and unlawful deprivation of liberty of any person.

contents.title: Part-2, Chapter-16: Offences Relating to Enforced Disappearance
contents.description: Criminalises enforced disappearance by state or non-state actors, consistent with international human rights obligations, and defines penalties and accountability mechanisms.

contents.title: Part-2, Chapter-17: Offences Relating to Kidnapping or Taking of Hostages
contents.description: Defines the offences of kidnapping, abduction, and hostage-taking, along with aggravated forms involving ransom demands or harm to victims.

contents.title: Part-2, Chapter-18: Sexual Offences
contents.description: Criminalises rape, sexual assault, sexual harassment, trafficking for sexual exploitation, and other sexual offences, including aggravated circumstances and protections for victims.

contents.title: Part-2, Chapter-19: Offence Relating to Medical Treatment
contents.description: Defines offences by medical professionals and facilities, including negligent medical treatment, performing procedures without consent, and fraudulent medical practice.

contents.title: Part-2, Chapter-20: Offences Relating to Theft and Robbery
contents.description: Covers theft, robbery, dacoity, and other property offences involving the taking of another's property, with graduated penalties based on the nature and value of the offence.

contents.title: Part-2, Chapter-21: Offences Relating to Cheating, Criminal Breach of Trust and Extortion
contents.description: Defines cheating, criminal breach of trust, misappropriation of property, and extortion, including offences by persons in positions of fiduciary responsibility.

contents.title: Part-2, Chapter-22: Offences Relating to Currency
contents.description: Penalises counterfeiting, forgery, and unlawful dealing in currency notes and coins.

contents.title: Part-2, Chapter-23: Offences Relating to Stamps
contents.description: Covers forgery and fraudulent use of government stamps and stamp papers.

contents.title: Part-2, Chapter-24: Offences Relating to Weights and Measures
contents.description: Penalises the use of false weights and measures in commercial transactions.

contents.title: Part-2, Chapter-25: Offences Relating to Documents
contents.description: Covers forgery of documents, using forged documents, fraudulent execution of deeds, and offences relating to the falsification of records and certificates.

contents.title: Part-2, Chapter-26: Offences Relating to Criminal Trespass and Criminal Mischief
contents.description: Defines criminal trespass, house-breaking, and criminal mischief including intentional damage to or destruction of property belonging to others.

contents.title: Part-2, Chapter-27: Offences Relating to Animals and Birds
contents.description: Covers offences relating to cruelty to animals, unlawful killing or harm to protected species, and illegal trade in wildlife.

contents.title: Part-3, Chapter-1: Offences against Privacy
contents.description: Criminalises violations of individual privacy including unlawful surveillance, interception of communications, unauthorised recording or publication of private information, and voyeurism.

contents.title: Part-3, Chapter-2: Offence Relating to Defamation
contents.description: Defines the criminal offence of defamation, distinguishing libel and slander, specifying defences available, and prescribing penalties for damaging the reputation of individuals.
